meta.title: Kharko Dozor — Open-source session replay
meta.description: Session recording and replay platform for the web. Free and open source, forever.

hero.headline: Watch how your users actually use your product
hero.subheadline: Kharko Dozor records real browser sessions with rrweb and lets you replay them in pixel-perfect detail. Self-hosted, framework-agnostic, free forever.

features.heading: What it does
features.subheading: Built on rrweb, designed for teams who want to understand their users without shipping their data to a third-party vendor.

sessionReplay.title: Pixel-perfect session replay
sessionReplay.description: Every DOM mutation, mouse move, and input event captured and reconstructed in a sandboxed iframe. Scrub, skip idle time, jump by page — the replay player treats long sessions as first-class.

trackedUsers.title: Tracked users and traits
trackedUsers.description: Link sessions to your end users through a simple `identify()` call. Traits, activity timelines, and per-user session history — all derived from your own SDK payloads.

slices.title: Slices for long sessions
slices.description: Sessions automatically split on navigation or idle gaps, each slice independently replayable. No more scrubbing through a 40-minute tab that was open in the background.

privacy.title: Your data, your database
privacy.description: No third-party processors, no hidden telemetry. Point the SDK at your own dashboard, host on your own Postgres, own every byte.

installation.heading: Install in under a minute
installation.subheading: Two packages on npm — pick the one that matches your stack. Full setup guides live in the SDK repo.
installation.step1: Create a project in the dashboard and copy the public key.
installation.step2: Install the SDK package that matches your stack.
installation.step3: Wrap your app (React) or call `init()` (vanilla) with the public key.
installation.docsLink: Read the full guide on GitHub

thankAuthor.heading: Thank the author
thankAuthor.subheading: Kharko Dozor is an open-source personal project. There are no paid tiers, no enterprise upsell, no plan to monetise. If it helped you and you want to say thanks, here are two ways.
thankAuthor.uahDescription: One-tap donation via Monobank link. Works from any card, any country, no sign-up needed.
thankAuthor.eurDescription: SEPA transfer to the author's EUR account. Use these details in your bank's transfer form.
thankAuthor.otherWays: Other ways to say thanks — or want to send from a different rail? <contactLink>Get in touch</contactLink>.

footer.tagline: Open-source session replay. Free for everyone, forever.
footer.copyright: © {year} Kolia Zamnyus. Kharko is an independent open-source project.
